title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Batters discusses the shooting of Mr. Cecil with Sir Oswald, noting that a man fled the scene after dropping a gun. Bardell explains that they only learned of the flight after Mr. Cecil informed them. Batters reveals he has men guarding the garden to catch anyone attempting to leave, though none have been caught yet. Batters expresses suspicion regarding the abundance of ivy vines at the location.

Sir Oswald is interrupted by his secretary, Rupert Bettman, who informs him that Mrs. Cutt is distressed and believes Sir Oswald might be murdered. Sir Oswald dismisses this and follows Bettman. Meanwhile, Bartlett and Jimmy discuss Bettman, whom Jimmy describes as smart but lacking a sense of humor. Miss Erin questions why Sir Oswald was wandering the garden at night, but the inspector defends Sir Oswald's character.

The investigation shifts to Miss Wade, who explains she was at the scene because she felt a need for security and had purchased a gun from Harrods. She claims she saw a tall man climbing down the ivy after a paper package landed at her feet. Detective Bates suggests the package was intentionally thrown at an accomplice. Mr. Cecil adds that the assailant was a strong, uneducated man who made growling sounds during a struggle.

Detective Balthazar discovers Countess Razkai unconscious behind a Spanish leather screen. As she regains consciousness with the help of Bender and Bondel, Bill expresses intense anxiety. The Countess assures the group of her well-being, noting her Hungarian "steel will," and directs a gentle comment toward Bill.
